injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Late May 2026; expected fit for tournament opener vs England on 2026-06-17

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Sustained multi-fragment fracture of left zygomatic bone in head clash with Locatelli; surgery on 2026-04-27

injuryUpdates.source: Al Jazeera, Goal.com, World Soccer Talk - Apr-May 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Captain. Age 40. AC Milan ruled him out for rest of Serie A season but surgery declared successful. Dalic publicly confident he will lead Croatia at the tournament. Likely to play in a protective carbon-fibre mask. Captain's status as starter is unchanged but match-sharpness is the concern after weeks without competitive minutes.

lastMatchPlayed.notes: First match back after 25-game absence; started at left-back, played 58 minutes in 3-0 win before being replaced by Ake

injuryUpdates.source: FOX Sports, Read Man City, Esteemed Kompany - May 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Returned to City training 2026-05-08 and made first match appearance 2026-05-13, ahead of original mid-May target. Guardiola said he is feeling good and should make a good tournament with Croatia. Major boost - he is Croatia's most important defender. Still needs minutes for full match fitness but availability is no longer in doubt.

lastMatchPlayed.notes: First Premier League start of the season since previous May; played 58 minutes

injuryUpdates.source: Read Man City, Manchester City reports - Apr 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: Completed long comeback from Achilles surgery and subsequent ankle injury. Lacks volume of minutes after a year mostly out, but is fit and selectable for Croatia. Generational midfield partner to Modric.

bettingOddsUpdate.toQualifyFromGroup: +350 to +400 (to win Group L; clear second favourite behind England at -335 to -350)
bettingOddsUpdate.notes: Tournament outright around +5000 at leading US books (~2% implied). Group L is widely treated as a two-horse race between England and Croatia, with Ghana (+1000) and Panama (+2500) priced as clear outsiders.

summary: The headline development is Luka Modrić's fractured left cheekbone suffered against Juventus on 2026-04-26, requiring surgery the next day and ruling him out of the Serie A run-in; head coach Zlatko Dalić insists the 40-year-old captain will lead Croatia at the tournament, likely wearing a protective mask. Counter-balancing that, Joško Gvardiol returned ahead of schedule from his January tibial fracture, training again on 2026-05-08 and starting at left-back for Manchester City on 2026-05-13, removing the major-doubt status that hung over Croatia's most important defender. Mateo Kovačić also completed his comeback from Achilles/ankle issues, while Croatia have played no internationals since 2026-04-16 (next friendlies vs Belgium on 2026-06-02 and Slovenia on 2026-06-07).
